name: i2c_master
description: I2C master controller with software-visible command/status registers and open-drain SCL/SDA controls.

source_refs.license: BSD-like OpenCores license in source header
source_refs.notes: Wishbone top-level register wrapper for I2C master.

source_refs.license: BSD-like OpenCores license in source header
BSD-like OpenCores license in source header
source_refs.notes: Bit-level SCL/SDA sequencer and arbitration handling.

parameters.name: DEFAULT_SLAVE_ADDR
parameters.description: Optional default slave address register value.

ports.name: scl_pad_o
ports.description: SCL pad output value, normally 0 for open-drain drive.

ports.name: sda_pad_o
ports.description: SDA pad output value, normally 0 for open-drain drive.

states.name: IDLE
states.description: Bus is free, waiting for command register start/read/write bits.

states.name: START
states.description: Generate SDA falling edge while SCL high.

states.name: BIT_TRANSFER
states.description: Shift address/data bits and sample ACK.

states.name: ACK
states.description: Handle acknowledge bit in either master read or write direction.

states.name: STOP
states.description: Release SDA high while SCL high.

states.name: ARBITRATION_LOST
states.description: Detected mismatch on open-drain bus while trying to drive.

constraints: SCL and SDA are open-drain style signals: drive low or release, never actively drive high.
Preserve start and stop timing relative to SCL high periods.
Expose arbitration-lost status when sampled bus state conflicts with the master drive intent.
Clear one-shot command bits when the byte controller acknowledges completion.
patterns: Separate software register decode from byte-level and bit-level protocol controllers.
Model SCL/SDA as open-drain: drive low or release, never actively drive high.
Clear one-shot command bits when byte transfer completes or arbitration is lost.
Latch status bits such as rxack, transfer-in-progress, bus busy, and interrupt pending.
implementation_notes: Keep register decode, byte controller, and bit controller as separate conceptual blocks.
Use prescale registers to derive SCL timing enables from the bus clock.
Represent command register bits for start, stop, read, write, ack, and interrupt acknowledge.
Latch status bits for rxack, bus busy, arbitration lost, transfer in progress, and interrupt pending.
test_strategy: Issue start/write/stop command sequence and observe SCL/SDA edges.
Inject NACK and arbitration-lost cases through pad inputs.
verification_goals: Wishbone writes update prescale/control/command registers at the documented addresses.
A write transaction produces start, address/data bits, ACK sample, and stop in order.
SDA changes only when allowed by the I2C phase except for start/stop conditions.
Arbitration lost and NACK cases are observable through status bits.
